mapping.a_element: steady-state mean fitness (largest eigenvalue of the mutation-selection evolution operator)
mapping.b_element: ground-state energy (lowest eigenvalue of the quantum spin-glass Hamiltonian)

mapping.a_element: point-mutation term, off-diagonal in genotype space (Crow-Kimura/Eigen)
mapping.b_element: transverse field, the off-diagonal spin-flip term

mapping.a_element: fitness / replication-rate landscape, diagonal over genotypes
mapping.b_element: random energy landscape, diagonal over spin configurations

mapping.a_element: maximum principle / variational solution for the mean fitness
mapping.b_element: mean-field solution for the ground state

mapping.a_element: selected/localized phase (population condensed on the fittest genotype)
mapping.b_element: phase where the eigenstate projects onto the unperturbed ground state

brief_justification: Both reduce to finding the extremal eigenstate of a mean-field/permutation-invariant spin Hamiltonian written as a diagonal landscape term (fitness in A, random energy in B) plus an off-diagonal field (the mutation operator in A plays exactly the role of the transverse field in B), obtained via a variational/maximum principle. Each shows a sharp transition at which the extremal eigenstate localizes onto a single peak/ground state (A's error threshold onto the sharp fitness peak, B's first-order quantum transition projecting onto the unperturbed ground state), with the sharp-peak landscape corresponding to the (quantum) REM.
